description: Alpinia caerulea, commonly known as native ginger or Australian ginger, is an understorey perennial herb in the family Zingiberaceae which grows in rainforest, gallery forest and wet sclerophyll forest in eastern Australia.
origin: AUSTRALIA, ASIA TROPICAL, PAPUASIA, AUSTRALASIA, NSW NS

description: Alpinia purpurata, commonly referred to as red ginger, ostrich plume and pink cone ginger, is a ginger native to Maluku and the southwest Pacific islands. In typical ginger fashion, A. purpurata is a rhizomatous plant, spreading underground in a horizontal growth habit, sending feeder roots downwards into the substrate and sprouting leafy vertical stems from nodes located along the rhizome.
origin: WIN GR, LEE BV, ECU, ASIA TROPICAL, CARIBBEAN
synonyms: ["Languas purpurata", "Alpinia grandis", "Guillainia novoebudica", "Guillainia purpurata", "Alpinia purpurata var. grandis", "Alpinia purpurata var. anomala", "Alpinia purpurata var. albobracteata"]

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun
care.tips: Native to: WIN GR, LEE BV, ECU, ASIA TROPICAL, CARIBBEAN

description: Alpinia zerumbet, commonly called shell ginger is native to eastern Asia. It is a rhizomatous, evergreen perennial that grows in upright clumps to 8-10’ tall. It more typically reaches 4-8’ tall in the greenhouse and 3-4’ tall as a houseplant. It is commonly called shell ginger because its individual shell pink flowers, particularly when in bud, resemble sea shells and its rhizomes have a ginger-like aroma. It is distinguished from other members of the ginger family by the fact that its flowers
origin: STH OO, EASTERN ASIA, HAW JI, PHI OO, JAP HK
synonyms: ["Alpinia schumanniana", "Alpinia rechingeri", "Alpinia penicillata", "Renealmia nutans", "Catimbium speciosum", "Languas schumanniana", "Costus zerumbet", "Languas speciosa", "Alpinia fimbriata", "Alpinia nutans"]

description: Turmeric, or Curcuma longa, is a flowering plant in the ginger family Zingiberaceae. It is a perennial, rhizomatous, herbaceous plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia that requires temperatures between 20 and 30 °C and high annual rainfall to thrive. Plants are gathered each year for their rhizomes, some for propagation in the following season and some for consumption or dyeing.
synonyms: ["Curcuma brog", "Amomum latifolium", "Curcuma ochrorhiza", "Curcuma longa var. vanaharidra", "Curcuma tinctoria", "Amomum curcuma", "Stissera curcuma", "Kua domestica", "Curcuma soloensis", "Curcuma domestica"]

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun
care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: seasonally dry tropical
care.tips: Similar plants: Canna | Native to: NCB, HAW JI, TON, MYA, MLY
care.used_for: ["Turmeric was first cultivated as a dye. It later became valued as a condiment and is the main ingredient in curry powder. In India", "Turmeric is used cosmetically. Medically", "the rhizomes are used in combination with other plants to treat headaches", "wounds", "snake bites", "stings", "sprains", "and fractures"]
care.used_for_details: Turmeric was first cultivated as a dye. It later became valued as a condiment and is the main ingredient in curry powder. In India, Turmeric is used cosmetically. Medically, the rhizomes are used in combination with other plants to treat headaches, wounds, snake bites, stings, sprains, and fractures
care.edible_parts: Turmeric is the main ingredient in curry powder. The rhizomes may be boiled, dried, peeled, and then ground to produce the spice.

description: Etlingera elatior is a species of herbaceous perennial plant in the family Zingiberaceae, native to the Malay Peninsular, the islands of Indonesia and New Guinea of the Malesia bioregion.
origin: LSI, VAN, TRT, PUE, WEST CENTRAL TROPICAL AFRICA
synonyms: ["Alpinia speciosa", "Cardamomum speciosum", "Geanthus speciosus", "Alpinia elatior", "Alpinia javanica", "Etlingera elatior var. pileng", "Alpinia magnifica", "Elettaria speciosa", "Nicolaia magnifica", "Nicolaia imperialis"]

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun

description: Hedychium coronarium, the white garland-lily or white ginger lily, is a perennial flowering plant in the ginger family, Zingiberaceae. It is native to the forest understorey of Asia.
synonyms: ["Hedychium coronarium var. chrysoleucum", "Hedychium coronarium var. maximum", "Kaempferia hedychium", "Hedychium flavescens var. chrysoleucum", "Hedychium maximum", "Hedychium gandasulium", "Hedychium album", "Hedychium prophetae", "Gandasulium lingulatum", "Hedychium chrysoleucum"]

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun

care.toxicity_note: Non-toxic to Dogs, Non-Toxic to Cats, Non-Toxic to Horses.
common_pests: Scale and spider mites can be troublesome, especially indoors.
care.used_for: ["The fragrant flowers are used for garlands."]
care.used_for_details: The fragrant flowers are used for garlands.

description: Hedychium flavescens is a perennial flowering plant from the Zingiberaceae. It is native to the Himalayas, Sichuan, and northern Vietnam, and naturalized in various other lands.
origin: INDIAN SUBCONTINENT, CPP WC, IND WB, AFRICA, COM
synonyms: ["Hedychium emeiense", "Hedychium panzhuum", "Hedychium coronarium var. flavescens", "Hedychium subditum", "Hedychium coronarium var. subditum"]

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun

description: Hedychium gardnerianum, the Kahili ginger, Kahila garland-lily or ginger lily, is a species of flowering plant in the ginger family Zingiberaceae, native to the Himalayas in India, Nepal, and Bhutan. It is an erect herbaceous perennial growing to 8 ft (2.4 m) tall with long, bright green leaves clasping the tall stems. The very fragrant pale yellow and red flowers are held in dense spikes above the foliage. They appear towards the end of summer.
origin: India, the Himalayan region   Southeast Asia
synonyms: ["Hedychium gardnerianum var. pallidum", "Hedychium pallidum", "Hedychium gardnerianum var. fasciatum", "Gamochilus speciosus"]

description: Cone-shaped flower spikes have a series of green or yellow bracts with translucent margins.  The blooms protrude from the bracts.  The inflorescence grows on a leafless stem that is separate from the foliage stem.  It can take a few years before a new plant will flower.

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun
care.light_guide: NC State: Partial Shade (Direct sunlight only part of the day, 2-6 hours)
care.edible_parts: Rhizomes, ginger root and young sprouts are all edible.

description: The white fruits is smooth and thin-walled.  The seeds produced are black ellipsoids.

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun
care.light_guide: NC State: Deep shade (Less than 2 hours to no direct sunlight)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Rhizome is used in tradional Asian cultures for food and medicine.
care.edible_parts: The rhizome can be used as a food flavoring.  The leaves and shoots are cooked and eaten as a vegetable.  Young flower spikes (without the bracts) can be eaten raw or cooked.
